workload: integrated schedule, rebuild, scheduler, equality, and container lanes

repo_state.notes: Coordinator synthesis from validated lane artifacts.

barrier_reasons: native action/rebuild regressions require rebuild-phase visibility boundaries
custom scheduler/backoff requires full match materialization and admission boundaries
synthetic native barriers in the overlap harness collapse the effective overlap window to 1

metrics.key_question: Do the real-barrier results reject an adapter framing or the new-backend framing?
metrics.overlap_survival_reason: The pure schedule lane overlaps cleanly, but native action, rebuild, container-refresh, and scheduler lanes depend on explicit visibility or materialization boundaries when native egglog remains authoritative.
metrics.performance_summary: No integrated new-backend runtime exists yet; component runtimes are recorded in source lane artifacts. Synthetic native barriers reduce the adapter-style schedule harness to stop/start behavior.
metrics.option_implication: Downgrade permanent middle-layer adapter duplication. Keep Option 3 open as a replacement-backend path if the next slice owns these barrier semantics without native shadow state.

verdict: exactness survives the tested native gates. The result rejects free overlap through native-authoritative barriers, but it does not reject a new backend that owns those barriers directly.
